TextENGNew: "...Here you go.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2150. It's just instant coffee, though."
"Oh... thanks."
Reina bowed her head, while dropping a sugar cube and some cream into the coffee handed to her in a paper cup, then gently blew on it and took a sip.
...It was fairly ordinary and not particularly tasty. Still, it was comforting on her thirsty throat after undergoing several hours of testing and answering so many questions.
"I'm sorry, I'm sure this has been a real pain for you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2152. ...Are you tired?"
"Just a little.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1218. ...But I'm fine."

"Thank you very much for your cooperation on this investigation.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2154. The information you provided us will be extremely valuable going forward.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2155. ...And of course, this is all strictly confidential."
"Right.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1220. ...I hope your investigation goes well."
Reina wrapped both her hands around the paper cup and savored the aroma as she bowed her head.
It was a very innocent and obedient gesture. ...There was no trace of the cruelty that Tomoe had witnessed in her before.
Reina knew about Natsumi's medication...
Upon learning that, Tomoe decided to drive over to NRIPS headquarters and speak with Chief Souma who she was on friendly terms with.
And when she found him resting in the laboratory break room, she somewhat forcibly requested that he analyze this drug and provide a counseling session for Reina Ryuugu.
"Y-you... Do you have any idea how insane this is?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1002. You need to follow procedures. You can't just demand things when it suits your convenience..."
"I'm well aware that this is an unreasonable request!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2157. But sometimes you need to bend the rules!"
She didn't even know if this could wait for another day. Plus, she couldn't call in a student like Reina very often.

Additionally, there was a possibility that information could be leaked like before, leading to more evidence being destroyed... so she had to do everything she could to prevent a worst-case scenario from befalling the girl.
Given those considerations, a rapid response was far more important than some notion of pride or common sense.
"Please, Souma-san!!"
"Geez, just this one time.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1004. I can't believe I've gotten mixed up with someone so outrageous..."
Yes, even though he bitched and moaned about it, Souma agreed to cooperate. Tomoe gave him a hearty "thank you" and then bowed to Reina in the next room over.
"Reina-san... I'm really sorry for pushing all of this on you so suddenly.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2160. But, I need your help."
"I need to find the truth behind this medicine... so I can prevent there from being any more victims!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2162. ...Please, I'm begging you for your cooperation!"
"...Detective."
Though Tomoe gave her a brief explanation of the situation in the car on the way here... Reina was confused by the rapidly developing situation and hesitated to reply for a moment.
Still, she sympathized with Tomoe's appeal and realized that Nagisa believed in this detective... so she raised her head and nodded.
"Understood.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1223. ...I'm not sure what I can do, but I'll do my best."
"Thank you...!"
...Then, Souma entrusted specialized researchers to analyze the drug Tomoe provided him, and immediately began counseling with Reina.
She interviewed on various topics, and subjected to blood tests, EEG readings, and more...
They had arrived at NRIPS before noon, but by the time they finished everything the western sky was already dyed in red...
"...So, Reina-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2165. Half a year ago, you didn't realize that I was watching you at the garbage mountain... is that right?"
"Right."
Reina gave a wholehearted nod. Her expression was stiff from the anxiety and tension, but her eyes were unwavering as she looked straight at Tomoe sitting in front of her, asserting her innocence.
"It's true that I've felt like people passing by were watching at me when I was playing at the construction site, on more than one occasion."
"But I've never felt the need to confirm if they were staring, and it's hard to recall faces of people you've never spoken to... so I don't really remember them."
"Well... that sounds reasonable."
Tomoe shrugged her shoulders and bitterly smiled while gulping down her coffee.
She able to recognize Reina Ryuugu at the garbage mountain because Ooishi had spoken about her right before that, and said that he was interested in her.
But as far as Reina was concerned, Tomoe was nothing more than a stranger.
Just as you forget the faces of the people you pass by on the road, it's hard to remember the faces of strangers you only see from a distance.
"Then, what about this man?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2168. He's a detective who contacted you about a month ago."
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1228.I'm sorry, I don't remember him.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1229. But I don't think I've ever spoken to a detective like him."
"...I see."
Tomoe took the photo back from Reina and let out a sign as she placed it back in her jacket pocket. ...As expected, that one fact was now confirmed.
"This detective told me that he informed you of Nagisa's death.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2171. ...But, that's clearly not the case."

"Right... I really didn't know about Nagisa-chan!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1231. I've never even come to Kakiuchi before!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1232. And, attacking a detective in a phone booth last year...? Absolutely not!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1233. That wasn't me!"
Was she lying to cover for it? ...Those doubts flashed through Tomoe's mind.
And yet at the same time, when she recalled the feeling around her neck... a strange sense of discomfort began to rise up inside Tomoe's chest.
(Then... did I actually get strangled at all?)
She thought back on how she felt while being strangled by Natsumi... and noticed that it felt different.
Just before she fainted half a year ago... she didn't feel agony, but an acute sharp pain.
And... that's right. An unusual itch, like something crawling out of her skin.
"...Come to think of it."
Immediately after waking up at the Irie clinic... there were scratches on Tomoe's neck.
It seemed reasonable to receive nail marks while being strangled, but scratches along the throat seemed... a little odd.
Plus... wasn't Reina Ryuugu standing outside the telephone booth in the first place...?
(Huh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2176. Wait a minute...)
Tomoe imagined the shape of the telephone booth... and realized that there was something strange about the timing of her being caught by surprise.

The door was hinged and had a full view of the outside through the glass. The area outside was illuminated, so if Reina was standing there she should've been able to see her before she could open the door.
And yet, she didn't notice that Reina was standing right in front of her until after the door was open...?
The facts weren't adding up, so deep in Tomoe's heart she shook her head at the idea.
Then... did she just dream that Reina was beyond the door? Was it a hallucination?
In theory, it didn't make a lot of sense. She didn't think she had the kind of mental illness that would make her confuse delusions and reality, and didn't even want to think about it.
But... it's true that Tomoe had been suffering from sleep deprivation and excessive fatigue from working hard every day, just as Dr. Irie had pointed out to her.
So, she decided to call Ooishi, and right after she did she started feeling her mind loosen up... and then...
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2179.Haah..."
She let out a big yawn. Her head was cloudy and her legs felt weak.
Ooishi would probably arrive 10... no, maybe 20 minutes after Tomoe called him.
"...Haah."
She leaned on the glass beside her and rested her cheek against it. ...It was cold, and somehow felt good.
Before long, it became difficult to keep her eyes open... and it felt like her cheek was being pulled upward as her field of view gradually shifted lower.
She only noticed that she had slumped down to the ground... after she was fully seated there.
There was a mild pain in her elbow. ...She must've bumped it somewhere.
Even so, her consciousness was sinking so far into the void that it didn't bother her at all...
And then... she quietly lost consciousness.
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2184.I see..."
If events from a dream she had after falling asleep intermixed with reality... all the reasoning would work out.
(But, that time...)
Just who was that strange girl that spoke to her right before she fainted...?
If it happened in a dream, it should've looked like someone she'd met before. ...But no matter how hard she thought about it, she couldn't remember seeing a girl like that.
Many questions still remained. ...But at least with regards to Reina, Tomoe seemed to have reached some level of understanding.
And, when Madoka's car exploded... she saw Reina at the scene.
That, too, was probably a misconception on Tomoe's part. She may have seen a "similar" looking girl and projected her beliefs onto that visage...
"...Got it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2187. I believe you."
At least, she couldn't spot any lies in Reina's testimony. And she couldn't believe her crying at Nagisa's grave was mere acting.

"I'm sorry. I've been suspicious of you for a long time.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2189. And on top of that, I even pressured you into this sudden counseling session...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2190. I forced you into such an uncomfortable situation... Really, I'm terribly sorry..."
"No... don't worry about it..."
Seeing a woman far older that her bowing so deeply to Reina caused her to be overwhelmed with awe.
Even when you're in the wrong, it's very hard and it takes a lot of courage to apologize to someone much younger than yourself... She completely understood that.
Reina suddenly remembered her mother, who she had driven away from her memories.
Why was it she gave up all her love for her and want to deny having her as a parent at all? ...She realized the reason.
(That's right.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1237. She... never apologized to us.)
Even until the very end... Reina's mother never apologized.
Even after betraying Reina's father and cutting off her relationship with Reina... she didn't offer a single word of apology.
Of course, the alimony payments they receive could logically be seen as a substitute for that.
But... what she truly wanted at that time was a proper apology to her and her father.
If she had new and important things she wanted to do, moving on was inevitable. Her mother was a talented woman, with her own values and her own life to live.
But... she should have at least offered an apology, even a single word, for making it impossible for things to go back to the way they were before.
For lying.
For betraying their trust.
To understand how much she hurt us by trampling on the precious family time we'd been living thus far... instead of simply running away from it.
She's an adult, so even if the person she wronged was a child she should sit up straight and accept it.
Even if she tried to maintain social appearances while doing so, she could be forgiven.
But instead, she chose to behave selfishly without showing a hint of kindness... More than anything else, that was the reason Reina couldn't forgive her mother.
(But... I have no right to be angry about that.)
After all... regardless of her reasons, Reina also ran away from her own problems in Ibaraki. She cast aside everything that used to be important to her and started a new life.
Even though she knew doing so would cause pain, trouble, and suffering for her old friends...
But... the detective standing in front of her acknowledged her mistakes and apologized... and even believed in her.
Though she was angry about it above all, she still regarded her like a dear friend...
(I want to be like that.)
Earnestly fighting to protect her loved ones. And becoming the sort of person who won't betray others next time. ...Yes, deep in her heart, Reina steadfastly swore that to herself.
"Thank you, Detective.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1242. And also..."
"What is it...?"
"Please don't let it end this way.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1244. ...Don't allow anyone else to make the same mistakes I made.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1245. ...I'm begging you."
"...Yes, I promise.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2193. I swear it, Reina-san... I mean, Rena-san."
Tomoe referred to Reina using her common nickname in Hinamizawa. When she heard that, she was stunned for a moment, staring back at her with her eyes open wide.
She wanted to throw away all the "i"cky things and be reborn as a new person... That was the strong wish behind that name.
At first it seemed like an aggressive way of running from her old self. ...But now, it seemed like it suited Reina best.
"Rena Ryuugu... I think that's a lovely name.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2195. I'd like to call you that from now on,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2196. if you don't mind?"
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1247.Sure."
Reina... or rather, Rena nodded her head and gave a bright smile.
(...I'm glad I came.)
It came with a lot of sad feelings. ...But this would give her the courage to take the next step.
Rena engraved those thoughts into her heart as she wiped the tears from her eyes...
After leaving Reina in the conference room by the lab, Tomoe headed to the medical exam room where Souma was waiting.
She found him smoking by the open window just outside the room.
"Smoking is prohibited in the exam rooms, after all.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1006. Sorry to do this in front of someone who dislikes smoking."
"Don't worry, I don't mind.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2198. ...So, what can you tell me about her symptoms?"

"It appears that... it's no different from a lull state.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1008. The medicine she was previously prescribed seems to suit her physically.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1009. An ordinary doctor would likely say there are no problems and suggest a wait-and-see approach."
"An... ordinary doctor?"

Tomoe sensed there was something behind those words, so a dubious look floated to her face. Souma crushed his cigarette butt in the ashtray by the window, then double-checked that there were no other people in the surrounding area before continuing in a whisper.

"She has something called latent trauma.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1011. When memories of past incidents put a lot of pressure on one's mind... they have an unconscious tendency to seek out escapist thoughts and actions."
"If not carefully checked, it can easily be mistaken for a positive and optimistic personality."
"Escapism...?"

"Right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1014. Even though doubts still linger in her mind, she tries to believe what she wants to believe.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1015. For example, it seems she'd decided that her aspirations and ideals for things to be the way she wants them to be are absolute."
"And they were very thin, but there were cut marks on her arm.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1017. When I asked about it, she said she had engaged in self-harm in the past."
"Self-harm...?!"
"She said there were many white insects running through her blood vessels, biting and tearing at her from beneath her skin."
"And she opened a wound with a knife in order to get that nasty, murky blood out of her system."
Souma lit another cigarette after saying that, then exhaled a puff of smoke out the window.
He was a foremost expert across all of Japan in the field of psychosomatic counseling. The fact that he was able to get that much information out of her was likely due to his exceptional skill.
(Anyway... what's this about bugs?)

Insects being present in the blood stream is not normally possible. If microorganisms got inside, white blood cells would activate and consume them. Or if that's still not enough, then blood clots would form, causing the body to undergo necrosis.
Thus, if there was a problem in the circulatory system... it would happen in the heart before the blood vessels.
"Does this delusion... well, I don't know if I should call it that, but is there a real condition that can make someone think they have insects in their blood vessels?"
"This is not the same as insects in particular... but there are cases of certain anxiety patients feeling that something is present in their blood vessels."
"Stress can cause touch sensory nerves to become hypersensitive, or for abnormal electric signals to be transmitted to the brain, which may cause the subject's pulse to feel stronger than normal."
"And in their subsequent confusion, it's not so unusual to think they'd project an illusion to fill in the gaps of their visual nervous system."
"And, if the subject had a prior experience like watching a horror movie or something like that, the possibility is even greater."
"...Confusion between memories and visual imagery?"
"Essentially, yes."
Come to think of it, she remembered.
When Natsumi was hospitalized after fainting in the park, she mentioned she was scratching at her throat because it was tingling like insects were inside.
And that impulse grew even stronger when Tomoe suggested she take her medicine.
In which case... maybe withdrawal symptoms of the drug can lead to self-harm? And Reina Ryuugu went through a similar experience...?
"This is... just an example, but..."
"Hmm?"
"Are there any drugs that can lead a patient to self-harm while taking them, or after the effects have expired?"
"There are."
Hearing that so readily confirmed, Tomoe was at a loss for words. Souma flicked his cigarette ash into the ashtray as he continued.
"Suicide is an action born of self-expression.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1028. ...We've discussed this before, haven't we?"

"Yes... I've asked about it in the past.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2208_a. You said that people under extreme mental stress may harm themselves to confirm the existence of their unstable ego, sometimes even destroying their lives."
Tomoe herself had suffered her own share of disappointment and despair... but she never wanted to die.
So, at one point she asked Souma for input, while trying to understand the thoughts of young people choosing to kill themselves out of desperation.
He explained to her about thoughts to keep the ego in check and actions of self-harm. ...At first glance these two ideas seem contradictory, but in fact they originate from a single idea.

"When something goes wrong in your interpersonal relationships, at work, or in your studies, you always feel that it's your fault.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2208_c. And a so-called inferiority complex is a form of self-punishment for that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2208_d. ...You told me that's the main factor driving self-discipline."
"Right. But... if their mental state is serious enough, sometimes their thoughts can progress even beyond that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1034. Into what we call "self-dissociation.""
"Regarding yourself... as a different person?"

"Or to put into other words, a desire to fully change yourself.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1036. To take the inferior part of yourself... the part you hate, and dissociate it from yourself as if it was a completely separate entity."

"And then the subject can create an ideal version of themself in their mind.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1038. In general, people with a strong sense of responsibility are more likely to fall down this path."
They hate to think that they hurt others, but they instinctively refuse to accept that someone else might be the cause of these unfavorable circumstances...

That certainly could be seen as a virtue, but when a child with that sort of personality suddenly begins to hold hatred toward others, they will be confused by the beliefs and values they always thought were absolute, and get lost in the self-contradiction.
Thus, they try to continue being a "good child" by denying these feelings of criticism for others, and refuse to accept that these dark emotions are their own.

As a result, they begin to have violent hatred for themself in the depths of their heart, and that feeling can eventually transform into "murderous intent" and a desire to deny their very existence...
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2211.So that's the psychological mechanism leading to suicide?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2212. And if so, is there medication that can drive them into that thought pattern?"

"Hmm... for example, anti-excitement drugs can affect the patient's ability to discern delusions from reality.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1040. And then, their improved mental communication ability can result in an increase in violent aggression, and their confused thoughts can make even themselves the target of attack.... or so it goes."
"Thus, this abnormal behavior that seems incomprehensible at first glance is actually instinctual behavior for a newly born personality trying to establish its own ego."
"In other words... a split personality?!"
That was what Souma was describing.

The more human beings are forced to live in a way they don't wish for, like being marginalized while growing up, will cause them to subconsciously develop dark emotions... Hatred, hostility, resentment, and more, often accompanied by a desire for destruction.

This is normally kept in check by the reasoning and emotions of their original personality, so they wouldn't act on those impulses without a strong trigger... but what if that balance was disrupted by medication they were taking?
"If anything, it's a kind of fantasy.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1043. For example, thoughts born from these dark emotions create another personality that the original person never possessed at all."

"And then the newly born personality will feel hostility toward the subjects who put them in their current situation.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1045. And furthermore, they'll continue to feel intense hatred for the original personality that continues to deny their existence."
"...Ultimately leading to acts of violence in their derangement.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1047. And finally... suicide."
While listening to Souma's explanation... Tomoe came to understand that this strange and seemingly incomprehensible series of events was in all likelihood triggered by realistic factors.
...The Oyashiro-sama that Reina spoke of and Natsumi was secretly afraid of didn't exist.
Everything was caused by drug-induced confusion and explosive impulses of emotion...!
"Thank you, you've been a huge help.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2216. With this and the results Matsuoka-kun is working on, the future of this investigation looks bright."
"Matsuoka... from Gifu prefectural police?"
"Yes, that's right... what about him?"
Souma and Matsuoka previously had a junior/senior relationship when they worked at the same place, so it was natural for him to immediately recognize the name.
...But, he frowned with a look on his face so pained that Tomoe could practically hear it.
"Souma-san...?"
"...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1050.Just give up on that material.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1051. It's probably far beyond your reach."
"Gifu prefectural police recently called me to file a complaint.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1053. They were upset that Matsuoka was analyzing a drug on my request, without consent of upper management."
"I had made similar requests in the past and he obediently obeyed, but I wondered what the hell was going on... So, that's what happened then."
"Wh-what do you mean?"

"The pressure you were worried about when you first came here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1056. ...That someone might have informed Gifu prefectural police that you asked Matsuoka to perform analysis for you."
"That's ridiculous!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2222. Who would do that...?!"
No... there's definitely a person who knows about both the existence of this drug and the fact that she asked Matsuoka to analyze it...!
She did her best to keep the secret watertight, but there was still a hole in an unexpected place...!
"Oh, but... why did Matsuoka-kun give them your name, Souma-san?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2224. I'm the one who made the request..."
"I'm sure he was trying to protect you.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1058. If he told them the truth, that you were the one who made the request... what do you think would've happened?"
...Indeed, it wasn't hard to imagine. It was completely obvious.
The Gifu and Aichi prefectural police departments have always had an antagonistic rivalry, so they've never gotten along.
In particular, the higher-ups at Oosawa station saw her as an enemy, and Deputy Chief Hata also had unfavorable views of her.
Given those circumstances, what would happen if they found out Tomoe had sent a request without following formal procedures? ...There was no need to even think about it.
So, Matsuoka made a quick decision to prevent Tomoe from being persecuted...
Tomoe slammed her fist down on the palm of her hand in bitter regret.
Thinking about all the trouble she caused for Matsuoka and everyone else, no amount of apology would be enough...
"...I'm terribly sorry for putting this on Matsuoka-kun.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2228. And you too, Souma-san. I've cause you so much trouble."
"If you really feel that way, then go out there and uncover the truth... Chief Inspector Minai.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1060. What more than that can you do?"
Tomoe caught her breath when she saw the strong gaze in Souma's eyes. His expression was silently rebuking her, telling her not to bother apologizing.

"Listen, Minai.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1062. We don't need your apology.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1063. No matter how harsh the punishment may be, it'll just mean less squandering our money for the next three months or so.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1064. ...But for you, it's different.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1065. You have a duty, so always be aware of that."
"Souma-san..."

"Absolute justice does not exist in this world.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1067. The deeper you dig, the more it becomes cluttered by beliefs and ties to other people. Even if you pursue it, you won't make it through."

"...But... no matter how slim the odds are, I want to believe in it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1069. The sensation... that we're fighting evil and delivering justice here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1070. And Matsuoka likely feels the same way."

"I actually received a fax from Matsuoka a little while ago with some partially complete drug analysis data.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1072. Taking some shortcuts based on those results, I might be able to put together a report by tomorrow evening.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1073. ...Leave the rest to me."
"...Right!"
Tomoe gave a nod and a salute, then turned around and headed down the hallway. Souma shrugged his shoulders and smiled as he watched her back.
"...I've gotten mixed up with some outrageous people.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_souma1075. I was planning to come in swinging, but she turned it around on me... Hahah."
Guess tonight's gonna be an all-nighter. ...Souma muttered that to himself as he returned to the lab.
He wasn't feeling tired at all though... In fact, he felt a clear sense of fulfillment for the first time in a long while.
"...Oh, Minai-san!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1074. There you are!"
Out of breath, Fujita burst into the First Investigation Division's office and didn't bother with a greeting as he rushed toward Tomoe, who was sitting in her old seat as Assistant Director.
...A day had passed since Tomoe had returned Reina Ryuugu to Hinamizawa.

On that day, Fujita had returned home after a long overdue absence, but when Tomoe contacted him and informed him that Souma had sent over drug analysis results, he quickly came back to Kakiuchi station.
(...I told him it could wait until tomorrow.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2234. His wife is going to be upset again.)
While still sympathizing with Fujita's wife... she secretly felt thankful that he placed a higher priority on his duties.
And she lamented her own shallowness for doubting him when she first heard that story from Yamaoki.
"So, how are the results?!"
"It's all right here... go ahead and read it."
As Tomoe said that, she handed him a bundle of copy documents that were sent over via fax.

Since Fujita had no background in pharmacy, he was skimming it with a look on his face like all the technical terms were going over his head... but terms in there like "unauthorized" and "grade-AA hazard" were enough to make him raise his head, looking astonished.
"H-hold on!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1077. What kind of medical institution would be handing out drugs like this?!"

"...Seems it was being conducted as part of a clinical trial, but based on what Natsumi-san told me, it was done without the patient's consent.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2237. I even doubted my eyes when I first saw that."
The analysis results of the drug Natsumi was taking were beyond Tomoe's expectations.
Souma stated that it had clear benefits for treating mental illness. ...But the main issues were the two items Fujita focused on: it was "unlicensed" and a "grade-AA hazard."

The latter in particular makes it subject to Narcotics Control Laws, which are even more strict than Pharmaceutical Affairs Laws. Violators will almost certainly be charged, and sentenced to up to ten years in prison.

Even medical personnel need to go through rigorous examination and procedures to obtain the necessary license to handle it, so it's not something a general hospital has the authority to prescribe to normal patients...!
"At any rate... what factors make this drug so dangerous?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1079. I realize that the component ingredients are hazardous, but..."
"There are synaptic cells in the body that control transmission between the brain and nervous system, and it seems this drug has a component that improves the efficacy of that ability."

"As a result, the speed of thoughts and information processing is temporarily increased, so the subject taking it is able to sort out their mental state...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2240. Long story short, anyone taking this medicine will become an instant saint."
Most causes of human stress occur when emotional thoughts can't be processed properly.
For example, when they have to accept the unintended consequences of their actions, such as setbacks, discord, or conflicts with others... naturally, people begin to feel bewilderment and regret.
And when they're forced to sort out and deal with those supressed negative emotions, they begin to feel lost, and suffer anxiety... That's stress.
That stress is usually relieved when a solution is found, and eventually disappears.
For example, by diverting your attention toward some other activity, like exercise or watching TV.
Or by organizing your thoughts through conversations with your family, friends, or lovers where you look back on your troubles.
Aside from that, worries can be settled as time moves on, when you forcibly process diluted information left in memory, and reflect on it in hindsight.
In doing so, most worries can be eliminated before they get any worse.
(...However.)
At times, people can end up developing negative thoughts and emotions as a result of their accumulating emotional distress, and find themselves unable to see a way out.

If the person has no hobbies they can distract themself with to turn their mood around, nobody to talk things over with, and ultimately no way to leave the problem behind, they'll stay trapped in their shell.
In a broad sense, that mental state can be described as "depression," and most of the mental illnesses in the world originate from that source.
The means to medically resolve that mental state is often through medical counseling, to assist the patient with unraveling their stagnant thoughts.
Or alternatively, to administer drugs with active components that improve neurotransmission capacity, so that the patient themself can resolve their mental problems.
"So basically, it means enhancing the patient's cognitive ability so the accumulated anxiety in their head can be swept away all at once?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2243. ...But the biggest problem is that this drug contains a significant quantity of mental transmission inhibiting agents."

"As a result, mental information they should be able to process and move on from instead lingers in their thoughts, and an amount of information beyond what can be controlled by reason accumulates in the patient's mind."
"...What does that mean?"
"Human reasoning acts as a checkpoint like you see in historical drama."

"Are the thoughts and feelings arising from their worries right or wrong according to common sense and morals? ...Reasoning plays a key role in judging their statements and actions, and correcting them accordingly."
"...But, what might happen when there is more information flowing through than the checkpoint can regulate?"
"...?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1083. Then, your emotions override your reasoning?!"
"It's a crude analogy, but essentially yes.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2249. Also, looking at the composition of the drug, it apparently contains anti-stimulants as well."
"Though this has the effect of calming the subject's outbursts of emotions, it also has the side effect of disrupting cognitive function and making it harder to distinguish delusions from reality."
In other words, no matter what outlandish results their thoughts and feelings may lead to, they can subconsciously interpret them as "just a dream."
It's not created from some crafty or cunning idea to justify themselves and avoid accepting any guilt. ...That thought process happens automatically and forcefully.

Thus, even when the time comes to take responsibility, the subject doesn't realize they've committed a crime at all, so often deliberation can be time consuming and the results can be controversial even after a conclusion is reached...
"...The more I hear about this medicine, the more ridiculous it sounds.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1085. It can turn good people bad? This is the devil's medicine."

"Also, quickly reaching emotional stability presents another danger.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2252. Taking medicine like that can naturally cause you to develop an addiction.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2253. ...In that sense, it's more like a narcotic."
Normally, negative emotions like anger, hatred, and resentment can be controlled by "reason."
But depending on a person's personality and the environment they were raised in, there are people out there who can't effectively control their emotions.
And from the components of the drug, it can be inferred that the problem is their propensity to "snap" in an emotional explosion that can't be suppressed.

In that case, it's something that should be corrected through education and interaction with friends... but when the symptoms manifested, the two of them would likely have taken this fast-acting drug to achieve mental stability.
However, the stronger the clinical effects, the greater the degree of dependence and subsequent withdrawal symptoms.
The so-called sudden emotional outbursts that are suppressed while taking the medication are immediately increased when the drug wears off.
In that moment, they feel elevated levels of paranoia, the urge to cause destruction, and otherwise extreme ways of thinking...
In other words, that temporary explosion of emotion presents an elevated risk of unexpected behavior.

"Souma-san writes that its effects are far too strong, so it should be classified as a dangerous drug across all of Japan.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2255. ...It seems it's even been banned in the West because it caused multiple deaths."
"Moreover, the developing self-loathing can even evoke self-harm...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2257. But, I think this clears up a lot."
Tomoe recalled the scene immediately before she was attacked by Natsumi in the park... when she stared at her with cruel eyes and scratched at her throat. Tomoe quivered just remembering it.
If she hadn't realized what was happening there and left Natsumi alone... she probably wouldn't have survived.
And she commended her own intuition for noticing that she was scratching her throat, even from that distance.
(Yeah... maybe that too...?!)
Tomoe suddenly thought of something. It was about the deceased nurse Youko Hiranuma, who was suspected of killing Kouhei Sawamura.
In that incident, Hiranuma seemed to commit suicide in resignation while surrounded by Honjou and other detectives from Ibaraki prefectural police.
But what if that was a similar case of "self-harm"...?
And what if someone deliberately gave her this drug beforehand...?!
(Then it's not a mysterious death or a suicide... it's murder!)

The evidence of that was the drug-like component found in her stomach. They suggested it may have been sleeping pills or motion sickness medication... but she didn't understand the details of the analysis.
But... what if someone had administered this drug to the nurse, and intentionally caused her to go out of control...?
The probability seemed high. She'd have to review the cause of death and autopsy charts again.
But... if that's the case, then who did it? And what on earth for...?
"So, what are you planning to do, Minai-san?"
"It's obvious, isn't it? It's Lowell Inc!"
"The report states that the medication's composition is very similar to Lowell Inc's trademarked drug, "Placil."@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1090. So, if we organize a raid there, then...!!"
"No good.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2263. ...We're not ready for that yet."
Tomoe immediately shot down Fujita's proposal.

That was the first thing that came to mind for her, too. ...But as long as the drug isn't licensed in Japan, Lowell Inc could simply deny that it was brought into the country and it would end at that.

"Additionally, a compulsory investigation into the Gogura university hospital would require a court issued warrant and explicit permission from the Ministry of Health and Welfare.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2265. ...But if information on this drug makes it to the Narcotics Control Office... then NCO won't take it quietly."
"If we do something wrong, it could turn this entire investigation sideways.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2267. NCO and the police have always had a famously bad relationship."
"Th-then all the more reason to do something!"
"The important thing isn't simply that this medication is dangerous.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2269. It's figuring out who on earth brought this danger here, and why."
"There's strong evidence that Lowell Inc or someone affiliated with them brought it into the country.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2271. ...You know a little about the previous Lowell incident, don't you?"
Fujita didn't know it as well as Tomoe, but he was familiar with the case.
At the time, they learned that human experiments were being conducted... but in the end, the higher-ups in the company couldn't be arrested.
Then, it would be necessary to determine who inherited Lowell Inc or its know-how, and find out how they may be connected to this hospital...!
"Then, what on earth can we do...?"

"At any rate, there must be some mastermind behind bringing this medicine here.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2273. That's the first thing we need to determine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2274. ...Investigation HQ is currently doing their best to scrub for the "material witness" related to that."
"What's wrong, Fujita-kun?"
"...@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1096.I... still can't believe it."
Fujita let out a despondent cry and bit his lip.
The "material witness" Tomoe referred to was the detective from Kakiuchi Station who had recently gone missing, Shirou Hanada.
In fact, he was at a loss for words when Tomoe told him that his direct superior Yamamura and even Yamaoki had lost all contact with him.
Fujita even shouted on the spot in agitation, "You bastard... I'll kill you!" He was so scattered that Tomoe had a hard time calming him down...
(But... there's no mistaking it.)
She found out about it when she contacted Kakiuchi Station after finishing up Reina Ryuugu's counseling at NRIPS.
That was when Tomoe first learned the police car she was riding exploded at the Kakiuchi service area...
"Exploded?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2278. What do you mean? You're saying there were explosives attached to the car?!"

"No... according to forensics, the fuel system was cracked and gas leaked into the car, so it reacted violently with some sparks from the cords on the radio, and that caused the explosion.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1098. They're saying it was a maintenance issue."
"...Impossible.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2280. That can't be true!"
Hanada was smoking the whole time they were driving to the Kakiuchi service area. If it was actually a maintenance issue, Tomoe's car would've exploded back when they were on the highway.
And what's more, could the weak electrical signal from a police radio really ignite a fire? Even if the insulated coating were peeled off, that seemed unlikely.
No... wait a minute.
There was a similar case... half a year ago. When she and Madoka were heading back after a meal, and a vandal broke inside and started the car...
"...So, is he safe?"
"Huh?"
"I'm talking about Hanada-kun!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2284. He drove the whole way to Gifu prefectural police with me, you know?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2285. Didn't he make contact?!"
"N-no...@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1101. Actually, Minai-san, are you sure you were with Hanada?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1102. Your name was the only one written on the vehicle scheduling board..."
...With that, Tomoe's suspicion turned to conviction.
The person who betrayed them... was someone she had always relied on, Shirou Hanada...!

"On top of that... there are inconsistencies in his account of the Reina Ryuugu investigation. Apparently, she didn't even know of Nagisa Ozaki's death.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2288. And she couldn't have been involved in the incident when I was in Ooishi-san's car half a year ago."
"Is that... all true?"
"There's no conclusive proof without hearing it from the man himself.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2290. But..."
Who could she trust now? ...Her judgment on that was now completely different than it was just a few days ago.
...If she hadn't followed Rena the other day.

She would have completely believed Hanada's report and continued to suspect her. As a result, even if he had interfered with Matsuoka's work, it would've been difficult for her to tell who was involved.
And furthermore, if Rena came to Kakiuchi and learned of Nagisa's death, she could be killed to silence her...
Even though it wasn't a pleasant thought, Tomoe couldn't help but feel relieved that they had avoided a worst-case scenario.
"...Anyway, we need to find out Hanada-kun's current whereabouts.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2292. Let's report to investigation HQ and make emergency arrangements."
"Understood.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1105. Then I'll report this to Yamamura-san, immediately..."
"Oh, before you do that... Fujita-kun?"
Fujita was already rushing out of the room, but on Tomoe's call he turned back and said "what is it?"
Then, Tomoe slowly walked up to him and...
Even holding back a little... she struck the side of Fujita's face with a good hard punch.
"Wh-what are you doing, Minai-san?!"
"Consider that your punishment for betraying me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2295. ...I was pretty hurt, you know?"
"Wh-what...?!"
Fujita didn't understand what she meant... but given the length of their relationship, he quickly realized it must be because of that "discussion" related to the case.
That damn Chief! Even though Fujita told him to keep it a secret from Minai-san...!
"W-well... umm..."

"I'm glad to know that you were worried about me... Really, I am.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2297. But, I'm not the sort of person to come this far only to leave things in the hands of others.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2298. So, let's do this together until the very end. ...Please."
"M-Minai-san..."
"Plus, if I'm the only one in a safe place, that'll only make things harder on you."
"Your wife might come storming into the station and yell at me, shouting "you put my husband in danger."@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2301. Sorry, but... I'd rather not have to deal with that."
"Ah, ahaha..."
Fujita was once again impressed.
And Yamaoki threatened him, "if you don't speak honestly, I'll tell your wife, got it?" ...Even though he gave in to the threat, it certainly was something he was concerned about, himself.
So, it actually felt refreshing to receive a blow from Tomoe.
"For now... there's still more to do.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2303. I'm counting on you, Fujita-kun."
"R-right!"
In any case, there was only one month left. ...And they'd spend that time pursuing this man, no matter what.
Fujita formed a slightly proud salute as he made that decision deep in his heart.
"That princess... really has done it."
"Indeed.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1002. ...Honestly, I didn't think she'd make it this far."
A man in a suit breathed a sigh of admiration, almost astonishment, as he poured some beer into Maeno's nearly empty glass.
It poured out a little faster than expected, so the table got splashed with the overflowing white bubbles, but Maeno didn't seem to mind and he drank it all at once.
"The words "I'd expect no less from Minai's daughter"... will go out of style.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1008. In fact, going forward, they'll be saying that "Tomoe Minai's father was a detective too.""
"Right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1004. Don't think of her as Minai the second, but the first... Her father was just the pioneer of the Minai name."
It was just a swap of the father/daughter order, but the man facing Maeno completely understood what he was getting at, and shrugged it off with a laugh.
The upper ranks of the police department viewed her as a sort of hereditary princess, but clearly that was a flagrant mistake. It might be a better example of a pupil surpassing her teacher.
(Seriously, that's amazing...)

They say that unexpected things often happen, but... who could expect it to go this far? Honestly, a case where NRIPS and prefectural HQ work together despite their cultural differences was a great honor.
"And it's not just about the police force. It sets a precedent for government offices in general.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1011. With this, it'll be easier to launch similar cases in the future."
"And the original purpose of this was to set up a forward operating base to allow profiling investigation methods to penetrate into the field..."

"But the princess didn't settle for a forward operating base. She directly laid siege to the castle.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1006. And it seems the enemies are scrambling to catch up.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1007. Just the other day, they tried to kill her in her patrol car."
"Indeed.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1014. The car bombing incident from half a year ago seemed like just a threat, but this time it was completely serious."
The bombing incident last year involving Tomoe and Madoka certainly was a disastrous accident that could have caused serious injury.
But the forensics team concluded that the smell of gasoline and the obvious handiwork would've made the situation clear to an attentive person like Tomoe.
"It's unfortunate that the car was destroyed by vandalism, but it would take some serious carelessness to start the engine without noticing those signs."

"In fact, the person who set it up might've been the one most frightened to see it happen?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1010. Thinking even though it was supposed to be just a threat, they might've gotten wrapped up in it?"
"Maybe so.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1016. ...But based on what I heard, the incident a few days ago was truly serious."

"C4... plastic explosives were cleverly set inside the power supply of the police radio, and that was the mechanism that triggered the fuel tank to explode.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1018. It was nothing more than luck that prevented this from ending in serious injury."
She'd get in the car and notice her companion wasn't there. And knowing Tomoe, she'd undoubtedly use the radio to report in to Kakiuchi Station while waiting for him...
Parking right in front of the gas station was a cunning choice. Even if she noticed the smell, she'd think it was normal given the location.
"In which case, this must be a pretty painful story for the enemy.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1012. And now they're rushing to erase their footprints, right?"
"We'll hold them off before they can do that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1020. ...Anyway, Akiyama,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1021. how far has Public Security gotten on this?"
The man called Akiyama drank his beer, then gently turned his gaze out the window... to check on his subordinates guarding the surrounding area.
Even among his longest trained subordinates, these were among his most trustworthy. And moreover, they were cleverly disguised so nobody would recognize them as policemen.

Additionally, the two of them were in a private room located farther away than any other, and Akiyama himself checked for the presence of listening devices and confirmed it was clean before entering the room.
...As a Public Security officer, he was especially cautious. And as a former member of Public Security, Maeno completely understood the need for those precautions.
"Before I deliver the report, Maeno-san,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1014. are you familiar with the Alphabet Project?"
"Alphabet Project...? What's that?"

"Called AP for short.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1016. It's based on the term ABC, which is an acronym for Atomic, Biological, and Chemical.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1017. You could call it a next-generation weapons research group."
"Right... I've heard about that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1024. That's the outdated research group that old-timer Koizumi set up by pulling strings and making threats, isn't it?"
Maeno looked exasperated as he muttered to himself "...That jackass."
Maeno was in the forefront as a Public Security investigator when the Lowell Incident came to light. For that reason, just speaking the name brought him anger and hatred.

"I... still can't believe that old-timer was involved in the Lowell Incident.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1026. But it's impossible to build a financial conglomerate like that so quickly without getting your hands dirty, especially several decades after the war."
"Plus, he was originally a military doctor.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1028. I wouldn't even be surprised to hear he was involved in data leaks and unethical experiments."

"Absolutely.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1019. Even so... in the end, the special investigation department at the Public Prosecutor's Office couldn't take him down.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1020. When he received a medal of honor before his death, everyone in Public Security seriously wanted to rise up and protest it."
"People like him deserve a bad death... and yet... he was able to live a full natural life.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1030. Haha, clearly God and Buddha don't exist."
"...I understand how you feel."
That wasn't lip service. Akiyama was speaking sincerely, while pouring out another glass of beer.
The Lowell Incident was one of the most shameful cases during Maeno's tenure at Public Security.
Not only did they fail to solve the case, but a senior detective who was like a brother to him was murdered during the course of the investigation... and they never managed to catch the culprit.
And what's more, the fixer directly dealing with the mastermind managed to evade arrest, and passed away as a hypocrite.
It was humiliating as a police officer, and a painful blow to the pride of someone as career-oriented as Maeno... Akiyama understood that because he had been in the same position.
No matter how many incidents you get involved in, the wounds of failure never completely heal. That dutiful sense of purpose was his reason to exist.

"Well, let's put that aside.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1032. ...These AP folks are those who can't forget the taste of war, and they're feigning the pursuit of defense and national interest to keep researching new weapons, right?"

"Nukes are absolutely out of the question, and biochemical development is prohibited due to international treaties.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1034. Actually accomplishing any of those seems doubtful.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1035. So, what about them?"

"So, the research old man Koizumi was pushing for in the later stages of his life was related to something called Hinamizawa Syndrome.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1023. ...Seems he made a huge investment in the research institute built in that lonesome village."
"Hinamizawa Syndrome...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1037. Is that some disease?"
"I heard it's a type of endemic disease.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1025. Apparently, parasites or viruses enter the hosts brain and take over their consciousness... with very noticeable symptoms."

"So, they're conducting research on a cure at the Irie Institute... or the Irie Clinic, as it's more commonly known.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1027. Some of the drug related data gathered there is being diverted to Lowell Inc to help with the development of Placil."
As he said that, Akiyama spread a document out on the table detailing the formula composition, with photos.
Aside from the color of the capsules, the composition was very similar at first glance... No, it was identical.
"...What does this mean?"
"It's what they call copy medicine.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1029. Earlier versions of the medicine completely stopped selling overseas after the incident in Japan."
"But at some point there was a need for an ataraxic drug, so a huge amount of research funding was invested into its improvement."
"However, if a drug with even higher efficacy were to appear, that would change the story a bit..."
"In other words... they plan to announce a new model of Placil before the competition can reach consumers."
Akiyama shrugged his shoulders to affirm Maeno's hunch. ...It was an egregious story.
"Well, this Hinamizawa Syndrome is a very specialized disease, so data from drugs developed specifically to treat it can't be used directly."

"Thus, they need to analyze the results and change the composition of ingredients to create a more versatile drug.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1034. And they're applying to the Ministry of Health and Welfare for approval to sell in Japan."
"But... Placil has a famously bad name in Japan.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1041. Even if it's an improved version, I don't think they'd ever be able to sell it under that name?"

"Right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1036. It's true that drugs are rarely approved on their first application... the members of the Pharmaceutical Review Board are likely pretty cautious after what happened 13 years ago."

"In fact, it seems they submitted an application to the board a while back, but were rejected in the end.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1038. And they requested the cooperation of medical institutions in various fields to conduct clinical trials, but those pleas were apparently all declined."
"Of course.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1043. Nobody wants to risk involvement in a fatal incident, but it's easy to imagine bribery going on."
In developing new drugs, it's common to ask hospitals and other organizations to conduct clinical trials to identify side effects that may not have been found in animal testing.
Of course, the content and the prescriptions are carried out in a careful manner, in an environment where an authorized doctor can vouch for its safety.
Since it requires the trust and cooperation of the doctor and the hospital as a whole to study a new drug, they're naturally reluctant to handle any high-risk items.
"But mind-altering drugs are especially valuable because they're difficult to handle, so they're a golden egg that can potentially bestow great fortune on success."
"And not just for medical purposes, but for recreational use, or even the military... There are many applications, and if the quality is good enough they can set any price they want."

"Thus, companies are so desperate to collect data, including rewards, that they're shooting all sorts of "live ammunition".@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1042. ...Complete research data like that is worth hundreds of millions."

"And so, they're seeking salvation through bribery.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1045. ...Then, all sorts of pharmaceutical companies must be drooling for this data?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1046. So, you think they'd use bribery to bypass the review?"
"No, the opposite.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1044. They're not lobbying it at all."
Ordinarily, survey data from authoritative doctors is mandatory for approval. But doctors with fame and renowned ability are always in high demand from pharmaceutical companies.
Thus, in order to obtain their cooperation, it's necessary to pay them a suitable reward and cover the research fees. And unaccounted-for funds may be included as well.
For that reason, it's nearly impossible to pass the approval process without "lobbying" the doctors involved.
"Ah... I see where this is going."
Maeno stamped the cigarette he was smoking into the ashtray as he said that.

"They're diverting data on the drug being studied at the Irie Institute, in order to make copy medicine.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1050. The drug is being prescribed to patients with the same illness... this Hinamizawa Syndrome, and conducting comparative tests."
"That way, they can identify problems and continue to improve it before commercial release.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1052. ...That's how it is?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1046. If the experiments are being conducted by AP, they don't even need to get a clinical trial license."
"You're saying... the application they submitted to the review board is just a dummy?"
"The Japanese people have developed a hypersensitivity to Lowell Inc products.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1048. So, they've already given up on selling in Japan."

"Instead, it would be better to sell in a different country with a larger population.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1049_1. So, Japan is nothing more than a test site in advance of proper commercialization.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1050. ...It's an utterly ludicrous story."
In all likelihood, the drugs developed and manufactured by Lowell Inc would be sold in foreign countries.
They were no longer thinking of entering the relatively small Japanese market. Their interest was primarily in collecting data and studying the drug in clinical trials.
(...Could those experiments have made it all the way to AP?)
It was a truly crafty, and yet deeply ironic plot. AP, a secret organization established for national defense and national interests, was providing the leg-work for the benefit of foreign businesses.
Maeno was tempted to grumble about how it was a masterful stroke of genius on the part of the traitor that thought it up.
"So... there's someone out there gathering this research data and running off with it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1056. ...But, who?"

"The intel still hasn't been confirmed yet.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1052. But, supposedly, after old man Koizumi's death the AP has been headed by Chief Cabinet Secretary Okuno, and the Minister of Health and Welfare Chiba."
"The researchers working below them like this Irie and Takano seem to devote their lives to studying this endemic disease, so they can safely be excluded..."

"So the wrongdoer is either Okuno or Chiba?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1059. Come to think of it, as long as the ruling party wins the next House of Representatives election Okuno is a shoe-in to be the next Secretary General..."

"The former Minister of Health and Welfare is the most fishy.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1054. He was originally manager of the pharmaceutical bureau.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1055. And right around that same time period, Shigeru Ounuma was arrested.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1056. ...They're far more than just wrongdoers, they're completely evil."
"Chiba, huh...? I see."
After hearing that, Maeno held his hand to his chin. Then, he pulled the notebook out of his inside pocket and flipped through it.
"Something come to mind?"
"Yeah.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1062. The Lowell Incident 13 years ago was a massive drug scandal."

"As you know, after several years of investigation many people were drowning in controversy, and the media was making a huge fuss about bribery and illegitimate reviews.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1064. ...But the truth is, there was another side to it."
"What do you mean?"

"You mentioned Ounuma's ties to the Lowell Incident. It's true that the Assistant Director of the Pharmaceutical Safety Bureau was arrested... but the information leading to that originally came from Lowell Inc, itself."
"It happened after parliament's last investigation into the issue, but thanks to that, the incident became far more widely known across Japan."
"If that information hadn't been released, it's possible that all of these domestic fatalities could have been buried in the darkness."
"It can't be... you mean...?!"
"Shigeru Ounuma was nothing more than a scapegoat. In fact, he had deep ties with Youhei Imamura... the former Minister of Foreign Affairs, and the goal was to weaken his influence."
"The Foreign Minister at the time had a strong voice and was trying to promote closer diplomatic ties with Asia, but that presented an obstacle to Western nations that were opposed to the idea."
"And the Director General of the Defense Agency, Okuno, favored diplomacy with the West.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1071. And he had close ties with Chiba."
In other words... the true objective of the Lowell incident was not to condemn the distributors of dangerous drugs, but to change the course of Japanese politics.

To that end, they deliberately brought dangerous drugs into Japan that would cause problems, knowing that bringing it to light would politically eliminate the opposition party members who were connected to it.
(They'd go so far...? Over foreign policy?!)

As expected, Akiyama was completely shocked when he heard the horrifying truth. Compared to modern politicians who look the other way against minor corruption when it benefits them, this was on a completely different level.
"Th...then, why is Chiba digging things up along that route?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1062. Why bring a scandal that buried your enemies back to the limelight?"
"...The scale is different.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1073. There's someone else, who isn't even working for the will of this country.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1074. You probably wouldn't even know about it."
"So, I think they were just lucky that Ounuma was arrested and they were saved.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1076. And they've already decided to start again."
"Ounuma wasn't actually the one who did anything... it was Chiba himself, acting under the name of the Assistant Director of the Pharmaceutical Safety Bureau."
"So, after Ounuma was arrested... by the time things started to cool down, he thought about starting it up again."
"...Compared to Placil, the amount of money coming into their pockets from this is an entire order of magnitude higher.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1080. How absurd. Heh heh heh...!"
Akiyama poured out another lukewarm beer, seeking to moisten his dried throat.

When it comes to matters Public Security is involved in, this level of international conspiracy isn't all that uncommon. ...Nevertheless, he was deeply shocked and felt ashamed of himself for his inexperienced response.
(I'd expect no less from the covert intel department of the National Police Agency...)
"...Minai-san might have gotten his hands on this."
"Never mind... it's nothing."
"...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1068.So, are you going to pass this info along to the princess?"
"We'll use Arakawa for that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1084. ...Minus the parts connected to Chiba, though.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1085. It's still too dangerous for that."
"Indeed..."
This was Tomoe. If she knew the full story of what Lowell Inc was up to this time, and that Chiba was the man lurking behind it, she wouldn't hesitate to arrest him.
But... when you're fighting against politicians, ordinary force simply won't cut it. In fact, getting caught up in something like that could even cost you your life.
Just like her father...
"Those folks may have already made contact.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1087. There's no way they haven't noticed the princess's activities and seen them as a roadblock."
"Should you act as a decoy?"
"Hah, don't be stupid.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1089. If I did that, Minai-san would curse me to death."
"Right."
Akiyama smiled and laughed, then used his lighter to light the cigarette Maeno held to his mouth. After a quick hand wave to show his gratitude, he exhaled a full puff of smoke out the window.
(Don't do anything rash, Princess.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1091. ...After coming this far, the enemy is not going to care about appearances anymore.)
Maeno pictured Tomoe's face, with that strong earnest conviction that was always in her eyes, and smiled to himself.
Even without her father, she was a bright woman. If possible, he wanted her to live a long life... and move ahead in the world. He clung to those selfless thoughts.
"...Well, that's why I need your help on this particular case.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1002. I'm really sorry to make you disobey a stop order from the higher-ups."
"Heh heh heh, a little late for that, isn't it?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1073. This is your favorite disciple, Maeno-san... that makes her like a younger sister to me."
"...I'll nail it into the director's head. No need to worry."
"Right. I'm in your debt.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1004. ...It would be nice if we didn't have to resort to that, but at this rate it's hard to expect that."
"Indeed. ...Oh, by the way."
"What is it?"
"There's something else.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_akiyama1077. About Chiba's second secretary, Tsukada... I found something quite interesting."
...The secret conversation between the two of them continued well into the night.
"...I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2305. So, the ghost of the Lowell Incident has been revived."
Tomoe bit her nails as she scanned through the documents Arakawa gave her inside the car.
The more she read, the more uncomfortable she felt, and it became harder to keep her anger and frustration in check.
"So... what did Maeno-san have to say?"

"Seems he's pursuing movements inside the Ministry of Health and Welfare.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1059. And Public Security is sending investigators to find a link between Lowell Inc and the Gogura university hospital."
"At last... things are really coming together."
"Oh... he did say one other thing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1061. "Don't do anything crazy.""
"What, he doesn't trust me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2309. Hehehe..."
After that, Tomoe opened the passenger side door and said "see you" as she stepped out.
Instead of a proper goodbye, Arakawa lightly beeped the horn at her, and then pulled out of the parking space at Kakiuchi Station.
"Now then..."
After stretching wide, Tomoe suddenly gazed up at the Kakiuchi Station building.
Since it was the dead of the night, there were very few windows with the lights still on. But one of the few still lit up was the Joint Investigation Headquarters meeting room.
...After Hanada's disappearance, Tomoe confided with the Joint Investigation HQ members about what she knew so far.

And even though it was unclear at the time who was leaking information, she apologized for the rudeness of secretly conducting an investigation of her own. Then she deeply bowed her head and asked everyone to lend her their strength once more.
"Come on, Princess, nothing has changed.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1032. If you're that careful, you're treating us like strangers."
Surprisingly though, Doujima, Yamamura, and the other investigators reacted by saying "so, what now?" ...Tomoe was expecting them to admonish her and that she'd have to dodge questions.

"You felt that something strange was going on. We felt it too.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1034. But if we had tried to identify the traitor as a group, it probably would've played out just as you said, Princess. It would've been a waste of time."
"...It is an amusing story though that you were right next to the traitor without even realizing it."
"...I'm ashamed of it."

"Oh no, take that as proof that we're up against an unconventional enemy this time.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1037. Now more than ever, it'll be dangerous unless we can step up.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1038. Right, everyone?!"
""Yeaaaaahhh!!""
All the investigators barked an answer to Doujima's question in unison.
Rather than losing morale, they all looked vibrantly energetic, dependable, and kind...
Tomoe gazed at them in awe, and savored the joy of being able to fight alongside them.
(Everyone... is really giving it their all.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2314. So I have to do the same.)
After muttering that to herself, Tomoe took a breath to raise her spirits, and then headed down the entrance hall toward the office of the First Division.
Then she stopped at the break room along the way to buy a can of unsweetened coffee... but that moment.
She could faintly hear a phone ringing.
And the sound grew louder as she approached the First Investigation Division room... Someone was calling the First Division.
"Is nobody in there...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2317. Geez."
Tomoe let out a sigh, then rushed to the nearby phone.
She thought about just hanging up on them... but fortunately, the voice coming in through the receiver was one of the night shift operators.
"Hello?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_jimuFa1002. Is Minai-san still there? There's a call coming in from an outside line."
"Yes?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2319. This is Minai speaking... Who might the caller be?"
"Oh sorry, I forgot to say. Umm... it's someone named Tsukada-sama.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_jimuFa1004. Do you know him?"
...She lost her voice.
Because there was only one person she knew with the last name Tsukada...
But...?
"Shall I patch him through?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_jimuFa1006. Or..."
"Oh, yes.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2322. Please send him through."
Then, the voice on the other end of the receiver switched from female to male... and she could hear the man say "Hello."
That voice was unmistakable...
"Hello...?"
"Ah, is that you, Minai?@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1002. I'm sorry, I didn't know how else to get in touch with you~."
"Tsukada...senpai?"
Hearing his voice was nostalgic.
Even though she had gone years without seeing him and only recently reunited with him... Tsukada's warm tone and the timbre of his voice were so distinct she could immediately identify him.
...However.
Right now... his voice felt somehow far away.
Her field of view started to narrow... and she could hear a high-pitched ringing inside her head.
"Sorry, I realize this is sudden.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1004. I got this phone number from Madoka-chan.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1005. And she told me you work at Kakiuchi Station."
"R-right... yeah."
...That was a lie.
Madoka didn't particularly care for Tsukada, so she never would've told him where to call. And besides, they had little to nothing in common, didn't they?
"Is something wrong?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2327. It's rather late at night, but did you need something?"
"Yeah... about that invitation from before...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1007. Would you like to go out to eat sometime this week?"
"...Go out to eat?"
"Yeah, I have something I'd like to discuss.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1007. What do you say?"
(...I see.)
Tomoe recalled where Tsukada was currently working, and quietly thought over what it might mean.
There was... indeed something they had in common. And if this phone call wasn't a mere coincidence, then...
"That's fine. Let's meet up.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2332. What's a good place to meet at?"
